As shown by Sam Johnstone’s sprawling save in stoppage time at The Hawthorns, Harry Maguire can carry a threat in the opposition box. But Manchester United’s plans have gone awry if he is on the ball more often than Bruno Fernandes in that rather important part of the pitch.

Maguire had six touches in West Bromwich Albion’s area on Sunday. Only Marcus Rashford, with seven, had more among United players. Fernandes had two, and though one of those was a sublime volley to claw a point against second-bottom Albion, the numbers tell a story of a disjointed display for Ole Gunnar Solskjaer’s side.

Centre-back Maguire had a cluster of those touches in two separate runs with the ball in the first half as United strained to break down the deep defence set by home manager Sam Allardyce. Taking the initiative, United’s captain dribbled effectively on both occasions – actually triggering a half-chance for Anthony Martial in the second instance...

Maguire had six touches in West Bromwich Albion’s area on Sunday. Only Marcus Rashford, with seven, had more among United players. Centre-back Maguire had a cluster of those touches in two separate runs with the ball in the first half as United strained to break down the deep defence set by home manager Sam Allardyce.
